verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a single-pass transmembrane protein with an extracellular ectodomain genuinely displayed on the outer leaflet of sperm cells. However, its expression and surface display are strictly restricted to the male germline lineage (spermatids/spermatozoa) and do not occur in somatic tissues. Per the instructions, germline-restricted surface display with its own anchor (TM) maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface, not yes. Surface accessibility is real within its native lineage but contextually gated to reproductive tissues.
